nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Valletort was born in Modbury, Devon, England as the daughter of Hugh Vautort and Lucy Brett. She married Richard Willoughby after 1342. She died after 1344, in Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Valletort est née à Modbury, Devon, Angleterre, fille de Hugh Vautort et Lucy Brett. Elle a épousé Richard Willoughby après 1342. Elle est décédée après 1344, à Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Valletort wurde in Modbury, Devon, England, als Tochter von Hugh Vautort und Lucy Brett geboren. Sie hat nach 1342 Richard Willoughby geheiratet. Sie starb nach 1344 in Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Valletort è nata il nel Modbury, Devon, England come figlia di Hugh Vautort e Lucy Brett. Lei sposato Richard Willoughby dopo il 1342. Morì dopo il 1344, nel Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth ValletortはイギリスデボンモドブリーでHugh VautortとLucy Brettの娘として生まれた。1342年以後にRichard Willoughbyと結婚した。彼女は1344年以後にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアノッティンガムで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Valletort(은)는 영국 데폰 모드버이에서 Hugh Vautort(와)과 Lucy Brett의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1342년 이후 Richard Willoughby(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1344년 이후 영국 노틴그함실르 노틴그함에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Valletort nasceu em Modbury, Devon, Inglaterra filha de Hugh Vautort e Lucy Brett. Ela casou-se com Richard Willoughby após 1342. Ela faleceu após 1344, em Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Valletort родилась в местности модбури, девон, Англия. Её родителей звали Hugh Vautort и Lucy Brett. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Willoughby после 1342 г. Она умерла после 1344 г. в местности ноттингхам, ноттингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Valletort nació en Modbury, Devon, Inglaterra como hija de Hugh Vautort y Lucy Brett. Se casó con Richard Willoughby después de 1342. Murió después de 1344, en Nottingham, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.
